mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (p > 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST coincides with negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during INDPRO drawdowns, or the signal shows no predictive power outside the specified inflationary regime.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation persisting above 3.0% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, sustaining higher discount rates that suppress S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are significantly positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.0, or the threshold fails to predict return direction (accuracy < 50%).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage growth that expands corporate revenue bases, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show a negative correlation with PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer momentum relative to aggregate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence condition coincides with positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable inflation signals healthy capacity utilization that reduces input cost pressures, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL growth < 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to separate return distributions significantly.
